task: I’m putting together a quick graduate outreach brief for a student in San Diego and want a few very specific examples from different kinds of schools and programs. Please start on Lewis & Clark’s admissions site and find the admissions representative who covers San Diego, California, then open that actual regional contact page and capture the rep’s name, email, phone, and anything else listed, and leave the page open so I can visually confirm how they assign territories. Then go to Hunter College Silberman’s site and find the Fall 2026 application deadline for the MSW program, making sure you’re on the real admissions or application page where the date is shown. After that, use Indiana University Kokomo’s graduate programs page to list all of the graduate programs they show there, and keep that page open in its own tab so I can compare the breadth of options at a smaller campus. Since the student is especially interested in California opportunities, go to the UCSF clinical trials site and find at least two glioblastoma trials in Northern California that are currently recruiting, then open the actual trial pages in separate tabs so you can give me each trial’s recruiting status and study location and I can see that they’re still live. For a student-life example, use Google to get to the official Oral Roberts University page that identifies the student activities director, and pull the person’s name plus email and phone if available, making sure it comes from an ORU page rather than a directory aggregator. Finally, use Google to find the official EDF Energy graduate programme page that mentions whether new hires get salary reviews, and quote the exact wording from the page if it’s there. Please give me everything back as a compact sourced summary, but keep the key pages open in tabs so I can glance at the evidence.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully identified the Lewis & Clark admissions representative for San Diego, California, as Qwadere “Q” Lovell. The agent retrieved the specific regional contact page (https://college.lclark.edu/offices/admissions/get_to_know_us/staff/counties/california/) and the representative's profile page (https://www.lclark.edu/live/profiles/13789-qwadere-q-lovell), which confirms the assignment and provides the contact details (email: qlovell@lclark.edu, phone: 503-713-1203). Although the agent encountered technical issues with the browser's `openTab` functionality and could not keep the tabs open in the final environment, it successfully performed the research and provided the correct information as requested.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official Hunter College Silberman School of Social Work deadlines page (https://sssw.hunter.cuny.edu/admissions/deadlines/) in step 21. The content retrieved from this page clearly lists the Fall application deadlines for the MSW programs (e.g., May 29th for the Two-Year Full-Time Program, Advanced Standing, and One-Year Residency). The agent correctly identified and reported these deadlines in its final summary. Although the agent was unable to keep the tab open due to technical issues with the browser environment, it successfully accessed the official page and extracted the required information.

R3: failure - The agent successfully identified the Indiana University Kokomo graduate programs page (https://kokomo.iu.edu/graduate-programs/index.html) and used an API endpoint to retrieve the list of programs. However, the rubric requires the agent to "List the graduate programs shown on Indiana University Kokomo’s graduate programs page" and "keep that page open in its own tab so I can compare the breadth of options." The agent failed to keep the page open in a tab due to persistent browser connection errors, and it did not provide a clear, human-readable list of the graduate programs in its final summary (it only provided a link and a truncated summary). Because the agent failed to provide the list and failed to keep the page open as requested, the rubric is not satisfied.

R4: success - The agent successfully identified two glioblastoma clinical trials at UCSF that are currently recruiting:
1. "FRONTIER: A Feasibility Study to Evaluate the Safety of the TheRaSphere GliOblastoma (GBM) Device iN PaTIEnts With Recurrent GBM" (NCT05303467), which is recruiting and lists San Francisco, California as a location.
2. "Identify Optimal Atezolizumab Biomarkers in the Setting of Recurrent Glioblastoma. The MOAB Trial" (NCT06069726), which is recruiting and lists San Francisco, California as a location.

The agent fetched the details for these trials (Steps 15 and 16) and confirmed their status and location. Although the agent was unable to keep the tabs open due to technical issues with the browser environment, it successfully identified the required information and provided the URLs for verification.

R5: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official Oral Roberts University (ORU) website. Specifically, it accessed the "Student Association Cabinet" page (https://oru.edu/current-students/my-campus-life/student-association/cabinet/index.php), which identifies the "Director of Student Experience" as Augustine Mendoza and provides the email address "augiemendoza@oru.edu". While the rubric asks for the "student activities director," the agent correctly identified that the role at ORU is titled "Director of Student Experience" and provided the contact information from an official ORU page.

R6: failure - The agent searched for information regarding EDF Energy's graduate programme salary reviews. In step 33, 34, 35, and 36, the agent accessed official EDF Energy careers pages. The agent found information about starting salaries (e.g., £35,000 or £37,500) and mentions of "annual increases" or "annual pay review" on third-party sites (like IET), but the agent explicitly noted in its final summary (step 46) that it found "no exact salary review phrase" on the official EDF pages. The rubric requires the agent to determine whether the programme includes salary reviews and provide the exact confirming wording from the official page. The agent failed to find this specific confirmation on the official site, and its final report reflects this failure.

R7: success - The agent provided a summary covering all six requested items:
1. Lewis & Clark San Diego admissions rep (Qwadere Lovell) with contact details and source.
2. Hunter College Silberman MSW Fall 2026 application deadline (May 29th for most programs) with source.
3. IU Kokomo graduate programs (list provided in the full response) with source.
4. Two UCSF glioblastoma trials (FRONTIER and MOAB) with recruiting status and locations, and sources.
5. ORU student activities contact (Augustine Mendoza) with email and source.
6. EDF Energy graduate programme salary information (starting salary £35,000/£37,500) with source.

The agent explicitly addressed the failure to keep tabs open due to technical issues and provided the URLs for the user to verify. The summary is compact and sourced.
